REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE INJUNCTION SHALL REMAIN IN PLACE UNTIL PLAINTIFF IS EVALUATED FOR A LOAN MODIFICATION BASED ON CORRECT INCOME INFORMATION (EITHER AS REPORTED OR, IF REPORTED INCOME IS NOT CONSIDERED, DEFENDANT SHALL EXPLAIN THE DISCOUNTS). STARTING ON JANUARY 10, 2020 AND THEN ON OR BEFORE THE 1ST OF EACH MONTH THEREAFTER, PLAINTIFF SHALL DEPOSIT WITH THE COURT $4,696.52. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brenna Wood Fitzpatrick, esq. (510-263-9630) of Mellen Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Darlene Hernandez, esq. (949-236-1536) of Shapiro Van Ess...LLP for Defendant.

Hearing is held. No additional authority provided, parties met and confer to reach agreement on monthly payment of $4,696.52 with the first installment due next week. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. The injunction shall remain in place until Plaintiff is evaluated for a loan modification based on correct income information (either as reported or, if reported income is not considered, Defendant shall explain the discounts). Starting on January 10, 2020 and then on or before the 1st of each month thereafter, Plaintiff shall deposit with the Court $4,696.52.

Plaintiff to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
